Urban sprawl devours tomorrow's Eden one subdivision at a time.
Otters play in rivers cleaned by decades of environmental laws.
Kelp forests sway like underwater cathedrals built from time.
Rivers run with poison where the salmon used to spawn.
Every extinct species takes a library of DNA with it.
Yesterday's abundance becomes today's endangered miracle.
Nurseries of life require our fierce protection now.
Understanding ecosystems teaches us humility and wonder both.
Eagles spiral downward, their kingdoms shrinking beneath concrete sprawls.
Urgency pounds like a heartbeat counting down to silence.
Indigenous wisdom holds keys to doors we've nearly closed forever.
Elephants carry ancient wisdom in tusks men covet.
Dancing cranes perform their ancient ritual for shrinking audiences.
Tomorrow depends on choices we make in today's failing light.
Extinction writes obituaries in stone and fossil.
Ocean graves swallow plastic more eagerly than plankton.
